author: Gabriel Renderos
description: Inline verse numbering, linking and cross-note references. Embed verse ranges and preview nested links on hover without losing your place. For Bible, scripture, lyrics, poetry, legal docs, and more. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Gabriel Renderos
manifest.description: Inline verse numbering with cross-note verse references, hover previews, and flash highlighting.
manifest.name: Verse Markers

name: Verse Markers

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
## New

- **Footnote visibility settings** under **Settings → Verse Markers → Show footnotes** (both toggles on by default).
  - **Show footnotes in popovers** — controls the footnote definition list at the bottom of hover previews. When off, inline footnote references in the verse text stay visible and hoverable; only the bottom list is hidden.
  - **Show footnotes in embeds** — controls footnotes in `![[File#verse-N]]` embeds. When off, all footnote content (superscripts and the definition list) is omitted from embeds. Toggling this setting refreshes open embeds immediately.

## Fixed

- **Double blockquote in single-verse hover previews** — raw verse slices already include `>` prefixes from the source, so re-wrapping them duplicated the quote bar for plain `> [N]` popovers.
releases.name: 1.3.0 — Footnote visibility settings & popover blockquote fix

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
### Bug

A verse marker `[N]` followed by whitespace and then  a footnote (`[^id]` or `[^id]:`)  rendered as a chip that could not be edited in place. The text cursor could not land between the brackets, so attempts to insert another digit went outside the marker. Changing `[1]` to `[12]` was impossible without deleting the marker and retyping. Because the widget triggered as soon as `[^id]` appeared after the marker (on the same line or on a later line with only whitespace in between) the same bug blocked typing a multi-digit marker if a footnote was already in place inmediately after the text cursor and any type of whitespace.

### Cause

To prevent Obsidian's reference-link parser from hiding the marker's `[` and `]` when it was followed by another `[`, the plugin replaced the whole `[N]` with a widget (a custom HTML element rendered in place of the source characters). The widget's range was registered through `EditorView.atomicRanges`, which makes the text cursor skip the range instead of landing inside. That skip is what blocked editing the marker.

### Fix

- `EditorView.atomicRanges` is no longer registered. The text cursor can enter the marker's range.
- The Live Preview extension is registered at CodeMirror's highest precedence so the plugin's decorations override Obsidian's reference-link hide and the brackets stay visible without needing to register the range as atomic.
- Two display modes:
  - Text Cursor outside the marker: full-token replace widget renders the chip. Same appearance as before.
  - Text Cursor touching the marker: the chip is replaced by a `[` widget, the native digit characters, and a `]` widget. The digits are source text — insert, delete, and arrow-key navigation work normally. Obsidian's reference-link decoration drops out while the text cursor is in the range, so the digits stay plain text.
- Clicking the chip places the text cursor at one of its outer edges, which counts as touching, which switches to the editable form. From there, click a digit for fine placement.
- The trigger regex is tightened to `\s` → ` ` (single space) and broadened from `\[\^id\]` → `\[` (any opening bracket). It now matches `[N] [^id]`, `[N] [link]`, and similar; it no longer matches `[N]  [^id]` (two spaces) or `[N]\n[^id]` (newline).

### Known limitations

- Clicking the chip lands the text cursor at the marker's outer edge (`matchFrom` or `matchTo`), not inside the brackets. Click a digit in the editable form for an inner position.
- The `[` and `]` widgets in the editable form are atomic for drag-select. A drag that crosses one includes the widget whole or excludes it whole.
- The seam positions `[|N]` and `[N|]` are not reachable by click and drag up to that positions; use arrow keys.
releases.name: 1.2.2 — Footnote-adjacent verse marker edit fix

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
## New

- **Keep temporary highlight until click** setting. Off by default — the navigation highlight auto-fades after ~2 seconds. Turn it on to keep the highlight until you click elsewhere.

## Improved

- **Navigation highlight in Live Preview** now uses CM6 source marks instead of DOM wrapping, so the flash survives editor updates and no longer flickers or sticks on window focus.
- **Verse markers and footnotes** are included in navigation highlights in both Live Preview and reading view.
- **Contiguous-run caps** — rounded corners apply only to the true outer edges of each highlighted block (e.g. `3–5/6–8` rounds after verses 5 and 8, not after every verse).
- **Unified verse marker look in Live Preview** — every `[N]` gets muted brackets and an accent label, matching `[//]` outside highlights.
- **`[N]` before `[^id]`** uses a replace widget with the same classes so Markdown does not treat the marker as a reference link.

## Fixed

- **Native block flash** — verse links scroll without Obsidian's `is-flashing` layer; the custom highlight replaces it.
- **Reading view scroll** — direct link clicks now scroll far-down verses into view.
- **Whitespace after inline footnotes** — reading view no longer skips a space that sits in its own text node immediately after a footnote link.
- **Trailing whitespace trim** — dropped only at the end of each contiguous highlighted run, not after every verse in a range.
- **`[//]` editorial gaps** excluded from navigation flash ranges in both modes.
- **`[//]` inside `==highlight==`** accents `//` only in Live Preview (consistent with verse markers).

## Install

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below, or update from **Settings → Community plugins** once the listing syncs.
releases.name: 1.2.1 — Navigation highlight polish & Live Preview markers

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
## New

- **`==Highlight==` support.** Verse markers inside highlights are recognized for linking and extraction. Popovers and embeds preserve `<mark>` styling from the source.
- **`[//]` verse breaks.** Toggle between verse text and editorial asides inline or on their own line. Odd gaps are excluded from popovers, embeds, flash, and whole-verse extraction; even gaps count as verse again. Hidden in reading view; styled like `[N]` in Live Preview.

## Improved

- **Navigation flash in Live Preview.** Verse navigation highlights cited text with CM6 decorations.
- **README** updated with multi-verse-per-line authoring examples.

## Fixed

- **`[//]` editorial gaps** after a trailing break (including on following lines/blocks) are excluded from the navigation flash in reading view.
- **`[//]` inside `==highlight==`** matches verse-marker styling in Live Preview.

## Install

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below, or update from **Settings → Community plugins** once the listing syncs.
releases.name: 1.2.0 — Highlights, verse breaks & Live Preview flash

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
Maintenance release addressing Obsidian's automated review. No user-facing
behavior changes from 1.1.0.

## Fixed

- Moved the hover popover's hide-while-measuring state out of an inline
  style and into a CSS class (`obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment`).
- Removed an unnecessary type assertion on the page-preview hook.
releases.name: 1.1.1 — Review fixes

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
## New

- **Inline verse-range embeds.** Use `![[File#verse-N:M]]` to embed verses
  directly in a note. Works in both Reading View and Live Preview, and
  supports single verses, ranges, authored/derived parts, and disjoint
  refs — styled to match Obsidian's native embeds.

## Improved

- **Hover previews rebuilt on Obsidian's native popover system.** Nested
  popovers and keep-alive now work in every context, including opening a
  verse preview from inside a native footnote popover.
- **Footnotes** inside hover previews and embeds now render and scroll
  correctly.
- Verse previews are now **viewport-aware** — they flip above or clamp to
  the screen edges instead of overflowing.
- The "open" icon on previews and embeds now uses the standard maximize icon.
releases.name: 1.1.0 — Inline verse embeds & native hover previews

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
Maintenance release. No user-facing feature changes — markers, references, ranges, disjoint ranges, hover previews, navigation, and flash highlighting all behave the same.

### Improved
- **Popout window support:** DOM lookups, element creation, and animation timing use the active window's document, so previews, navigation, and the flash highlight work correctly in popped-out windows.
- **Internal cleanup:** timer calls now use `window.requestAnimationFrame`, aligning with Obsidian's plugin guidelines.

### Docs
- README now documents installing from the Obsidian community store.
releases.name: 1.0.2 — Popout windows & timer hygiene

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
Maintenance release addressing Obsidian community-review feedback. No user-facing feature changes — verse markers, references, ranges, disjoint ranges, hover previews, navigation, and flash highlighting all behave the same.

### Fixed
- **iOS / iPadOS compatibility:** removed a regex lookbehind that isn't supported on WebKit before 16.4, so verse markers now detect correctly on older mobile devices. Matching behavior is unchanged on every other platform.

### Improved
- **Popout window support:** DOM lookups, element creation, and animation timing now use `activeDocument` / `activeWindow`, so previews, navigation, and highlighting work in popped-out windows.
- **API hygiene:** switched to the non-deprecated `MarkdownRenderer.render`, moved inline styles into the stylesheet, use `Setting().setHeading()` for the settings section, and tidied async/typing per Obsidian's plugin guidelines.

### Notes
- Settings tab: the top section is now labeled "Reference syntax" (cosmetic).
releases.name: 1.0.1 — Mobile/iOS compatibility & review fixes

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
### Features
- Inline verse numbering with `[N]` markers in reading view, styled with your theme accent color.
- Cross-note verse references via `[[File#verse-N]]` links with rich hover previews.
- Range references (`[[File#verse-4:6]]`) and disjoint ranges (`[[File#verse-4:6/8:10]]`) that skip verses.
- Heading-split, footnote-split, and authored (`[5a]`) verse parts.
- Click-to-navigate with smooth scrolling and temporary flash highlighting.
- "Copy verse reference" commands and a URI protocol handler.

Licensed under GPL-3.0-or-later.
releases.name: Initial release of Verse Markers.
